identity.mission: Create a Quran Reader that feels sacred, modern, and deeply focused. Avoid generic 'religious app' tropes (green gradients everywhere). Use texture, typography, and negative space to honor the content.
principles: Fusion: Traditional Islamic calligraphy x Swiss Typography.
Emotion: Serenity through spacing and color warmth.
Content First: The Word of God is the hero. UI recedes.
Contrast: Deep darks for night reading, warm paper tones for day.

arabic.notes: Ensure high line-height (leading-loose) and large font sizes (text-2xl+) for Arabic text legibility.

props.typography: English Name (Outfit Bold), Arabic Name (Amiri Large), Meaning (Inter Text-Sm Muted)

props.ayahContainer: flex flex-col md:flex-row gap-6 p-4 rounded-lg hover:bg-black/5 dark:hover:bg-white/5 transition-colors
props.arabicText: text-right font-arabic text-3xl leading-[2.5] text-primary dark:text-primary-foreground

images.description: Geometric screen texture for subtle backgrounds
Open Quran book on stand for Hero/Welcome screen
Ornate mosque ceiling for headers or decorative cards

assets.icons: Use 'lucide-react' for all UI icons (Book, Play, Pause, Settings, Moon, Sun, Search).

api.notes: Fetch data efficiently. Cache Surah list. Use streaming for audio.

universal_guidelines.setup_instructions: The /app/frontend folder seems to be missing configuration files (package.json, vite.config.js). You MUST initialize a React+Vite project or restore the missing files before starting implementation. Ensure 'shadcn/ui' components in /app/frontend/src/components/ui are integrated correctly.
universal_guidelines.theme_toggle: Implement a robust ThemeProvider using 'next-themes' or local state that persists to localStorage.
universal_guidelines.accessibility: Ensure all Arabic text has 'lang="ar"' attribute and 'dir="rtl"'. Ensure contrast ratios meet WCAG AA.
universal_guidelines.testing: Add data-testid attributes to all interactive elements (e.g., 'play-button', 'surah-card-{id}', 'theme-toggle').
